skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through reviewing, verifying, and responding to incoming pull request feedback before making changes.
skill.summary: Helps evaluate code review feedback before changing a pull request.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance indicators. Manual review found these are false positives in markdown examples and prose; no executable code, network access, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged fenced text blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are markdown examples and workflow guidance, not executable scripts or commands run by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are inside SKILL.md prose or fenced text examples. No script file, command runner, or shell invocation exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not define or call cryptographic algorithms. Line 3 is the skill description, and line 141 is an example response about fixing review feedback.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive computation at these lines. The hits are ordinary text.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line advises using technical reasoning when responding to code review feedback. It contains no network scanning command, host enumeration logic, or external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain language guidance in a markdown document. No evidence found of network reconnaissance behavior or network-capable code.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incorrect, or broader than the requested change. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify feedback before acting.
actual_capabilities: Restates reviewer feedback as concrete technical requirements.
Prompts verification against the current codebase before changes are made.
Separates unclear review items from actionable fixes.
Guides reasoned pushback when feedback is technically wrong.
Prioritizes multi-item feedback by risk, simplicity, and complexity.
Encourages testing each review fix before moving to the next item.
limitations: It does not fetch pull request comments by itself.
It does not replace human judgment on product or architecture decisions.
It depends on the assistant having access to the relevant code and feedback.
It provides workflow guidance, not automated review enforcement.

use_cases.title: Clarify Reviewer Requests
use_cases.description: Turn vague or multi-part review comments into clear questions and concrete next steps.

use_cases.title: Evaluate External Feedback
use_cases.description: Check whether contributor or reviewer suggestions match the project context before accepting them.

use_cases.title: Control Review Scope
use_cases.description: Keep requested changes focused when feedback expands beyond the pull request purpose.

prompt_templates.title: Understand Review Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to read these review comments and restate each requested change in clear technical terms before making edits.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Ambiguous Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to identify which review comments are unclear, which are actionable, and what questions I should ask before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Technical Correctness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to verify whether these reviewer suggestions are correct for this codebase, including compatibility, existing usage, and regression risk.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Review Response Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build a response plan for these requested changes, including accepted fixes, pushback points, tests, and implementation order.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks to remove legacy handling, but the code may still support older platforms.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend checking supported platform targets, explaining the compatibility concern, and asking whether to fix the legacy path or drop support.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks for several changes, but two items are unclear.
output_examples.output: The skill would separate understood items from unclear ones and recommend asking for clarification before partial implementation.

output_examples.input: A reviewer suggests building a large feature around an unused endpoint.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend checking real usage first, then asking whether the endpoint should be removed instead of expanded.

best_practices: Verify each review comment against the codebase before changing code.
Ask focused questions when feedback is unclear or scope-changing.
Test each accepted fix before moving to the next review item.
anti_patterns: Blindly accepting review feedback before technical verification.
Implementing only the easy parts while unclear items remain unresolved.
Expanding scope because a reviewer requested a professional-looking feature.

faq.question: Does this skill write code changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides how an assistant should evaluate and respond before making code changes.

faq.question: Can it help with GitHub pull request comments?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the relevant comments and code context, then ask the assistant to apply the workflow.

faq.question: Does it always recommend pushing back on reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It recommends pushback only when feedback is unclear, incorrect, risky, or outside the agreed scope.

faq.question: Is this useful for small typo fixes?
faq.answer: Usually no. Simple and unambiguous fixes do not need this workflow.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill require network access?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown instruction skill and does not include network-capable code.
